name: Golden Circle (Why / How / What)
summary: Simon Sinek's keynote arc: lead with belief, follow with method, finish with proof. Best for brand stories, mission-driven keynotes, and any deck where the audience needs to feel why before they can evaluate what.

beats.name: Why
beats.description: Open with the belief or purpose that animates the work. Why does this exist, and why should anyone care beyond commerce? State it plainly and emotionally — the why is meant to be felt before it is understood.

beats.name: How
beats.description: Translate the belief into the principles that guide how you operate. The values, decisions, and trade-offs that make your approach distinctive. The 'how' is what people who share your why recognize as familiar.

beats.name: What
beats.description: Show the products, services, or outputs that the why and how produce. Concrete examples, not abstractions. The what is the artifact of the belief — proof that the why is more than rhetoric.

beats.name: Proof
beats.description: Demonstrate the impact in lives, customers, or outcomes. Stories beat statistics here; specific people and moments earn the audience's belief better than aggregate numbers. End with proof that what you do works because of why you do it.

beats.name: Invitation
beats.description: Close by inviting the audience to share the belief — to buy, build, join, or evangelize. The strongest invitations don't argue; they assume alignment and offer a way in.
